title: Shiny Module Tree Utility
text: goal modtrees create plain-text tree views module namespace hierarchy statically parsing R source files.

title: Shiny Module Tree Utility
text: can install development version modtrees like :

title: Shiny Module Tree Utility
text: Example using ttdviewer source code files: Use mod_tree() function print module namespace tree:
code: library(modtrees) R ├── app_server.R ├── app_ui.R ├── ctr.R ├── inspect_plot.R ├── launch.R ├── load_tt_data.R ├── logr_msg.R ├── mod_input.R ├── mod_list.R ├── mod_plot.R ├── mod_report_desc.R ├── mod_report_download.R ├── mod_report_input.R ├── mod_table.R ├── render_report.R └── testthat.R  1 directory, 16 files mod_tree(   path = "R",    app_fun = "launch",    ui_fun = "app_ui",    server_fun = "app_server"   ) █─launch ├─█─app_ui │ ├─█─mod_input_ui │ ├─█─mod_report_input_ui │ ├─█─mod_report_desc_ui │ ├─█─mod_report_download_ui │ ├─█─mod_list_ui │ ├─█─mod_table_ui │ └─█─mod_plot_ui └─█─app_server   ├─█─mod_input_server   ├─█─mod_report_input_server   ├─█─mod_report_desc_server   ├─█─mod_list_server   ├─█─mod_table_server   ├─█─mod_plot_server   └─█─mod_report_download_server

what: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
title: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
text: build_tree() performs depth-first traversal call graph starting given node, producing nested list structure suitable rendering.

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
text: node Character scalar; root function name. call_graph Named list element character vector function names called function. visited Character vector already-visited node names (used internally prevent cycles).

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
text: nested list elements name (character) children (list similar structures).

what: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
title: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
text: extract_func_def() hecks whether parsed expression top-level function assignment (e.g., name <- function(...) { ... }) returns function name body.

title: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
text: expr parsed R expression.

title: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
text: list elements name (character) body (expression), NULL expression function definition.

what: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
title: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
text: find_calls() recursively walks parsed R expression collects names functions appear either direct calls (fun()) bare name references (fun).

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
text: expr parsed R expression. known_names Character vector function names look .

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
text: Character vector unique matched function names.

title: Hello, World! — hello
text: Prints 'Hello, world!'.

title: Hello, World! — hello
Hello, World! — hello
code: hello() #> Error in hello(): could not find function "hello"

what: Display a Shiny function module tree — mod_tree
title: Display a Shiny function module tree — mod_tree
text: mod_tree() statically parses R source files directory, detects Shiny modules (via calls shiny::NS() shiny::moduleServer()), builds call graph, prints plain-text tree showing module hierarchy.

title: Display a Shiny function module tree — mod_tree
Display a Shiny function module tree — mod_tree
text: path Character scalar; path directory containing R source files. Defaults "R". app_fun Character scalar; name top-level app launch function. Defaults "launch". ui_fun Character scalar; name UI function. Defaults "app_ui". server_fun Character scalar; name server function. Defaults "app_server".

title: Display a Shiny function module tree — mod_tree
text: Invisibly returns nested tree structure (list). tree printed console side effect.

title: Display a Shiny function module tree — mod_tree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mod_tree("R") mod_tree("R", app_fun = "run_app", ui_fun = "ui", server_fun = "server") } # }

what: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
text: render_tree() takes nested tree structure (produced build_tree()) returns character vector lines using box-drawing characters style lobstr::ast().

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
code: render_tree(tree, prefix = "", is_last = TRUE, is_root = TRUE)

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
text: tree nested list name children elements. prefix Character scalar; current indentation prefix (used internally recursion). is_last Logical; whether node last sibling. is_root Logical; whether node root.

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
text: Character vector formatted lines.

Launch the demo Shiny app — run_demo

title: Launch the demo Shiny app — run_demo

title: Launch the demo Shiny app — run_demo

what: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
title: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
text: uses_ns() recursively walks parsed R expression detect calls shiny::NS() shiny::moduleServer(), reliable indicators function Shiny module.

title: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
text: expr parsed R expression.

title: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
